validation_summary.evidence: python -m py_compile on sync_harness.py exits 0 (~0.1s); python -m unittest skills.development-harness.scripts.tests.test_sync_harness -v passes 8/8 (2.1s); python -m unittest discover skills/development-harness/scripts/tests passes 169/169 up from 164 (35.3s)

checkpoint.summary: unit_024 complete. sync_harness.py now reports three fleet-drift divergence types alongside its existing phase-report analysis: orphan_worktree (on-disk .harness/worktrees/<batch>/<unit>/ dirs with no fleet entry), stale_fleet_entry (fleet entries whose worktree_path is missing on disk), orphan_branch (harness/batch_*/<unit> branches without a matching fleet entry). Each divergence carries the batch_id + unit_id needed to feed into teardown_batch or re-dispatch. Three new private helpers: _list_on_disk_worktrees, _list_harness_branches (silently skips when git unavailable), _detect_fleet_drift. run_sync loads state.json (optional) and appends fleet-drift findings to divergences. 5 new TestFleetDriftDetection cases + 3 existing sync tests all green. Test suite 164 -> 169.
checkpoint.next_action: Complete unit_025 (PHASE_005): new skills/development-harness/scripts/tests/integration/test_parallel_invoke.py -- builds a fixture git repo, sets up a 3-unit phase, runs dispatch_batch then shell-scripted fake agents that commit canned files in each worktree, then merge_batch. Asserts final state, clean worktree removal, and no residual harness/batch_*/ branches. This is the PHASE_005 closing unit; after it merges, open the phase PR.
